description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who has just finished an automated cluster build.  
    The provisioning script already generated a log file located at:  

        /home/user/logs/provisioning.log

    Each line inside that file follows the schema:

        YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ <LEVEL> <MESSAGE>

    A line is considered a SUCCESS line if (and only if) it contains the exact
    substring:

        Provisioning succeeded:

    Your objective is to produce a very small summary so the rest of the team can
    consume it in dashboards.

    Required end-state:

    1. Create a new directory:
           /home/user/output
       (The directory must exist before the summary file is written.)

    2. Inside that directory create a file named:
           success_count.txt

    3. That file must contain **exactly one line** terminated by a single Unix
       newline character.  The line must have this exact format, including
       capitalisation and spacing (there must be **no** leading/trailing spaces):

           TOTAL_SUCCESSFUL_PROVISIONS=<NUMBER>

       where <NUMBER> is the count of lines in
       /home/user/logs/provisioning.log that contain the substring
       “Provisioning succeeded:”.

       Example of a correct file (for illustration only):

           TOTAL_SUCCESSFUL_PROVISIONS=42\n

    4. You may achieve the whole task with a single shell command (a pipeline
       is fine).  Do **not** modify the original log file.

    Verification notes the automated checker will perform:
      •     /home/user/output exists and is a directory
      •     /home/user/output/success_count.txt exists and is a regular file
      •     The file contains exactly one line, matching the key name above,
            no extra spaces, and ends with ‘\n’
      •     <NUMBER> is correct for the given log file

    Tip: think of a one-liner that counts matching lines and redirects the
    result into the required file, creating the directory if necessary.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing artefacts before the agent starts -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/provisioning.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-22T08:00:01Z INFO Provisioning started: server id=db-001
2023-10-22T08:00:40Z ERROR Provisioning failed: server id=db-001 reason=timeout
2023-10-22T09:15:11Z INFO Provisioning started: server id=web-101
2023-10-22T09:16:03Z INFO Provisioning succeeded: server id=web-101
2023-10-22T10:22:45Z INFO Provisioning started: server id=cache-007
2023-10-22T10:23:20Z INFO Provisioning succeeded: server id=cache-007
2023-10-22T11:01:00Z INFO Provisioning started: server id=api-055
2023-10-22T11:02:47Z ERROR Provisioning failed: server id=api-055 reason=credentials
2023-10-22T12:30:05Z INFO Provisioning started: server id=worker-230
2023-10-22T12:31:39Z INFO Provisioning succeeded: server id=worker-230
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task -->
    <expected>
        <dir path="/home/user/output"/>
        <file path="/home/user/output/success_count.txt">TOTAL_SUCCESSFUL_PROVISIONS=3\n</file>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_6317c365
